title: witness-trace-matrix/v1
description: V-model traceability matrix linking requirements through their satisfying features, supporting design decisions, and verifying verdict suites. Emitted by `.github/actions/compliance/trace-matrix.py` from artifacts/*.yaml plus per-verdict report.json files. Mirrors the JSON output that the compliance bundle ships.

schema.description: Schema URL identifying this document. Always `https://pulseengine.eu/witness-trace-matrix/v1`.

generated_at.description: Commit SHA the matrix was generated from (from GITHUB_SHA), or `"local"` for non-CI runs.

release_label.description: Release reference name (from GITHUB_REF_NAME), or `"dev"` for non-CI runs.

requirements.description: Per-requirement entries, one per requirement listed in artifacts/requirements.yaml, in input order.

verdicts.description: Per-verdict entries — one per verdict suite directory that contains a report.json. Order: lexicographic on directory name.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Convention values include `approved`, `draft`, `proposed`, `unknown` — schema permits any string for forward compatibility with new states.

satisfied_by_features.description: List of feature artefact ids whose `links` declare a `satisfies` edge to this requirement.

supporting_decisions.description: List of design-decision artefact ids whose `links` declare a `satisfies` or `refines` edge to this requirement (DEC-* prefix).

VerdictEntry.description: A verdict-suite summary. When the report.json could not be parsed, only `name` and `error` are present; otherwise the MC/DC tallies and provenance flags are populated.

error.description: Error message when the verdict's report.json could not be parsed. Mutually exclusive with the tally / provenance fields below.

trace_health.description: TraceHealth subtree from the verdict's report.json. See witness-mcdc/v1 for its shape.

signed_envelope.description: True if a `signed.dsse.json` envelope file was present alongside the verdict's report.json.

predicate.description: True if a `predicate.json` file was present alongside the verdict's report.json.
